---
title: "Bayesian Model"
author: "Derek Kusmenko"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
# Load libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(brms)
library(janitor)

# Beaches stations
beaches_stations <- c(7692, 7317, 7318, 7365, 7427, 7315, 7695, 7364, 7314, 7316, 7428)

# Ontario holidays
toronto_holidays <- as.Date(c(
  "2021-01-01", "2021-02-15", "2021-04-02", "2021-05-24", "2021-07-01", "2021-08-02", "2021-09-06", "2021-10-11", "2021-12-25", "2021-12-26",
  "2022-01-01", "2022-02-21", "2022-04-15", "2022-05-23", "2022-07-01", "2022-08-01", "2022-09-05", "2022-10-10", "2022-12-25", "2022-12-26",
  "2023-01-01", "2023-02-20", "2023-04-07", "2023-05-22", "2023-07-01", "2023-08-07", "2023-09-04", "2023-10-09", "2023-12-25", "2023-12-26",
  "2024-01-01", "2024-02-19", "2024-03-29", "2024-05-20", "2024-07-01", "2024-08-05", "2024-09-02", "2024-10-14", "2024-12-25", "2024-12-26",
  "2025-01-01", "2025-02-17", "2025-04-18", "2025-05-19", "2025-07-01", "2025-08-04", "2025-09-01", "2025-10-13", "2025-12-25", "2025-12-26"
))

# Data preprocessing
bikes_raw <- read.csv("01_Data/beaches_bikes.csv")
bikes_raw$Date <- as.Date(bikes_raw$start_date_actual)

# Classify trips
bikes_classified <- bikes_raw %>%
  mutate(
    Starts_In_Beach = Start.Station.Id %in% beaches_stations,
    Ends_In_Beach   = End.Station.Id %in% beaches_stations,
    Trip_Type = case_when(
      !Starts_In_Beach & Ends_In_Beach ~ "Inbound",  # Visitors arriving
      Starts_In_Beach & !Ends_In_Beach ~ "Outbound", # Locals leaving
      Starts_In_Beach & Ends_In_Beach  ~ "Internal", # Moving within the BIA
      TRUE                             ~ "External"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(Trip_Type != "External") # Drop data completely irrelevant to the Beaches

# Aggregate daily trip counts
daily_data <- bikes_classified %>%
  group_by(Date, Trip_Type, User.Type) %>%
  summarise(
    Total_Trips = n(),
    Year = first(year),
    Month = first(start_month_name),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Day_of_Week = wday(Date, label = TRUE),
    Is_Weekend  = ifelse(Day_of_Week %in% c("Sat", "Sun"), 1, 0),
    Is_Holiday  = ifelse(Date %in% toronto_holidays, 1, 0),
    
    # Convert categorical variables to factors
    Month        = as.factor(Month),
    Year_Numeric = as.numeric(Year) - 2021, 
    Year         = as.factor(Year),          
    Is_Weekend   = as.factor(Is_Weekend),
    Is_Holiday   = as.factor(Is_Holiday),
    Trip_Type    = as.factor(Trip_Type),
    User_Type    = as.factor(User.Type)
  )


Toronto_Daily_Temp <- read_csv("01_Data/Toronto_Daily_Temp.csv")
Toronto_Daily_Temp <- clean_names(Toronto_Daily_Temp)
Toronto_Daily_Temp <- Toronto_Daily_Temp |> filter(local_year > 2020)

# parse date so it is compatible with the bike share data
temp_clean <- Toronto_Daily_Temp |> 
  mutate(
    date = as_date(local_date),
    month_name = month(date, label = TRUE)
  ) |> 
  select(date, mean_temperature, min_temperature, max_temperature, total_precipitation) |>
  # sort by the new datetime column
  arrange(date) |> 
  filter(!is.na(date))

# join temperature data to bike share
daily_data <- daily_data |>
  inner_join(temp_clean, by = c("Date" = "date"))

# standardize continuous features
daily_data$Temp_Scaled <- scale(daily_data$max_temperature)
daily_data$Rain_Scaled <- scale(daily_data$total_precipitation)

# define bayes model
formula <- bf(
  Total_Trips ~ Trip_Type * User_Type * (Temp_Scaled + I(Temp_Scaled^2) + Rain_Scaled + Is_Weekend + Is_Holiday) + Month + Year_Numeric
)

# define priors
priors <- c(
  prior(normal(0, 5), class = "b"),               # Prior for betas
  prior(student_t(3, 0, 10), class = "Intercept"), # Prior for the intercept
  prior(gamma(0.01, 0.01), class = "shape")        # Prior for Negative Binomial
)

# run mcmc
bia_model <- brm(
  formula = formula,
  data = daily_data,
  family = negbinomial(), 
  prior = priors,
  chains = 4,             
  cores = 4,              
  iter = 2000,            
  warmup = 1000,          
  seed = 123              
)

# model summary
summary(bia_model)
```

```{r}
# plots to ensure model convergence
plot(bia_model)

# compare simulated data to real data
pp_check(bia_model, ndraws = 100)

# conditional effects plots
plot(conditional_effects(bia_model), ask = FALSE)

```

```{r}
# setup to view specific plots to get expanatory results
visitor_condition <- data.frame(
  Trip_Type = "Inbound",
  User_Type = "Casual Member"
)

# how temperature effects inbound casual riders
plot(conditional_effects(bia_model, 
                         effects = "Temp_Scaled", 
                         conditions = visitor_condition), 
     ask = FALSE,
     main = "How Temperature Impacts Inbound Casual Riders")

# how rain effects inbound casual riders
plot(conditional_effects(bia_model, 
                         effects = "Rain_Scaled", 
                         conditions = visitor_condition), 
     ask = FALSE,
     main = "How Rain Impacts Inbound Casual Traffic")

```

```{r}
# For forecasting/prediction, set up covariates as necessary
future_conditions <- data.frame(
  Temp_Scaled = c(1.5, -0.5), # Assuming 1.5 is ~25°C, -0.5 is ~10°C
  Rain_Scaled = c(0, 5),
  Is_Weekend = as.factor(c(1, 0)),
  Month = as.factor(c("Oct", "Oct")),
  Year_Numeric = c(3, 3), # 2024 is year 3
  Is_Holiday = as.factor(c(0,0)),
  Trip_Type = as.factor(c("Inbound", "Inbound")),
  User_Type = as.factor(c("Casual Member", "Casual Member"))
)

# Predict the counts with 95% credible intervals
predictions <- predict(bia_model, newdata = future_conditions)
print(predictions)
```

```{r}
# checking test statistics of real data to replicated sets
plot_max <- pp_check(bia_model, type = "stat", stat = "max", ndraws = 500) +
  labs(title = "Can the model predict the busiest days?",
       subtitle = "Vertical line = Actual busiest day | Histogram = Model's guesses for the busiest day")
print(plot_max)
```

```{r}
# PPC Density overlay
plot_density <- pp_check(bia_model, type = "dens_overlay", ndraws = 50) +
  labs(title = "Posterior Predictive Check: Overall Distribution",
       subtitle = "Dark line = Actual Data | Light lines = Model Simulations")
print(plot_density)
```

```{r}
# PPC Density overlay for different trip types
plot_grouped <- pp_check(bia_model, type = "dens_overlay_grouped", 
                         group = "Trip_Type", ndraws = 50) +
  labs(title = "Model Accuracy: Inbound vs. Outbound vs. Internal")
print(plot_grouped)
```

```{r}
library(tidybayes)
library(posterior)
library(dplyr)

# view proportion of sampled coefficients that are positive or negative
posterior_samples <- as_draws_df(bia_model)

# view what proportion of rain covariates are negative
# if 100% then know rain always results in less riders
head(select(posterior_samples, b_Intercept, b_Rain_Scaled))

prob_rain_negative <- sum(posterior_samples$b_Rain_Scaled < 0) / nrow(posterior_samples)

cat("There is a", prob_rain_negative * 100, "% probability that rain reduces traffic.\n")
```

## Time Series Model

```{r}
library(tidyr)

# Create a combined grouping variable 
daily_data <- daily_data %>%
  mutate(TS_Group = paste(Trip_Type, User_Type, sep = "_")) %>%
  mutate(TS_Group = as.factor(TS_Group))

# Pad missing dates
# If a day is missing, it gets filled with Total_Trips = 0
daily_data_ts <- daily_data %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  complete(Date = seq.Date(min(Date), max(Date), by = "day"), 
           TS_Group, 
           fill = list(Total_Trips = 0)) %>%
  mutate(
    Day_of_Week = wday(Date, label = TRUE),
    Is_Weekend  = ifelse(Day_of_Week %in% c("Sat", "Sun"), 1, 0),
    Is_Holiday  = ifelse(Date %in% toronto_holidays, 1, 0),
    Month       = as.factor(month(Date, label = TRUE)),
    Year        = as.factor(year(Date)),
    Year_Numeric = as.numeric(as.character(Year)) - 2021
  )
daily_data_ts <- daily_data_ts %>%
  arrange(TS_Group, Date)
```

```{r}
# Time series formula
ts_formula <- bf(
  Total_Trips ~ Trip_Type * User_Type * (Temp_Scaled + I(Temp_Scaled^2) + Rain_Scaled + Is_Weekend + Is_Holiday) + 
                Month +  User_Type * Year_Numeric + ar(time = Date, gr = TS_Group, p = 1)
)

# Update Priors
ts_priors <- c(
  prior(normal(0, 5), class = "b"),
  prior(student_t(3, 0, 10), class = "Intercept"),
  prior(gamma(0.01, 0.01), class = "shape"),
  prior(uniform(-1, 1), class = "ar")
)

# Split the data based on the Year
train_data <- daily_data_ts %>% filter(Year %in% c("2021", "2022", "2023", "2024"))
test_data  <- daily_data_ts %>% filter(Year == "2025")

# Train the model ONLY on the 2021-2024 data
train_model <- brm(
  formula = ts_formula,
  data = train_data,
  family = negbinomial(),
  prior = ts_priors,
  chains = 4, cores = 4, iter = 2000, warmup = 1000, seed = 123
)
```

```{r}
# model summary
summary(train_model)

plot(train_model)

# compare simulated data to real data
pp_check(train_model, ndraws = 100)
```

```{r}
library(tidybayes)
library(tidyr)

# Create a copy of the test data
test_data_modified <- test_data

# Duplicate the date column
test_data_modified$Real_Date <- test_data_modified$Date

# Overwrite Date to numeric
test_data_modified$Date <- as.numeric(test_data_modified$Date)

# Drop NAs
test_data_modified <- test_data_modified %>% drop_na()

# Generate predictions for 2025
predictions_2025 <- test_data_modified %>%
  add_predicted_draws(train_model, incl_autocor = FALSE) %>%
  group_by(Real_Date, Total_Trips) %>% 
  summarise(
    Predicted_Mean = mean(.prediction),
    Lower_95 = quantile(.prediction, 0.025),
    Upper_95 = quantile(.prediction, 0.975),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rename(Date = Real_Date)
```



```{r}
library(dplyr)

daily_collapsed_predictions <- predictions_2025 %>%
  group_by(Date) %>%
  summarise(
    # Sum the actual reality
    Total_Trips = sum(Total_Trips),
    
    # Sum the model's average expected guesses
    Predicted_Mean = sum(Predicted_Mean),
    
    # Sum the confidence intervals 
    Lower_95 = sum(Lower_95),
    Upper_95 = sum(Upper_95),
    
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# View cleaned output
head(daily_collapsed_predictions)

ggplot(daily_collapsed_predictions, aes(x = Predicted_Mean, y = Total_Trips)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, color = "darkblue") +
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1, xlim = c(0, max(daily_collapsed_predictions$Total_Trips, na.rm=T)), 
              ylim = c(0, max(daily_collapsed_predictions$Total_Trips, na.rm=T))) +
  labs(
    title = "Actual vs. Predicted Traffic (2025)",
    subtitle = "Dots ABOVE the red line are days the model Underestimated",
    x = "What the Model Predicted",
    y = "What Actually Happened"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}
# Check if the ACTUAL traffic fell between the model's Lower and Upper bounds
coverage_test <- daily_collapsed_predictions %>%
  mutate(
    Captured_Reality = ifelse(Total_Trips >= Lower_95 & Total_Trips <= Upper_95, 1, 0)
  )

# Calculate the percentage
coverage_percentage <- sum(coverage_test$Captured_Reality) / nrow(coverage_test)

cat("Our 95% confidence intervals successfully captured reality", 
    round(coverage_percentage * 100, 1), "% of the time in 2025.\n")
```

```{r}
# Calculate the residual for every single day
daily_collapsed_predictions <- daily_collapsed_predictions %>%
  mutate(
    Residual = Total_Trips - Predicted_Mean,
    # Create a label for plotting later
    Error_Type = ifelse(Residual > 0, "Underestimated", "Overestimated")
  )

# Calculate the overall Mean Bias Error
mbe_score <- mean(daily_collapsed_predictions$Residual, na.rm = TRUE)

cat("Mean Bias Error:", round(mbe_score, 1), "trips per day.\n")

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(daily_collapsed_predictions, aes(x = Date, y = Residual, fill = Error_Type)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", size = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Underestimated" = "darkred", 
                               "Overestimated" = "steelblue")) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 month", date_labels = "%b") +
  labs(
    title = "Model Bias Over Time (2025)",
    y = "Actual minus Predicted Trips",
    x = "Month",
    fill = "Model Behavior"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```
